claims.claim_text: The SolarWinds supply-chain compromise showed that a signed artifact is only trustworthy when the producing path is controlled, observable, and independently verifiable.
claims.span_hint: line 30 | section: Why This Module Matters
claims.rationale: CISA documents the SolarWinds Orion software supply-chain compromise and related signed malicious software lifecycle artifacts.

claims.claim_text: Log4Shell was a Log4j vulnerability that required broad mitigation work across affected organizations and downstream products.
claims.span_hint: line 32 | section: Why This Module Matters
claims.rationale: CISA's joint advisory identifies Log4Shell as CVE-2021-44228 in Apache Log4j and gives mitigation guidance for vendors and affected organizations.

claims.claim_text: In OCI registries, image manifests are identified by content-addressed digests and tags are human-readable references.
claims.span_hint: line 88 | section: 2. Establish Artifact Identity with Digests and Signatures
claims.rationale: The OCI image manifest specification is the upstream reference for image manifests and content-addressed artifact identity.

claims.claim_text: Cosign signs and verifies OCI artifacts in the Sigstore ecosystem.
claims.span_hint: line 106 | section: 2. Establish Artifact Identity with Digests and Signatures
claims.rationale: The Cosign project README documents container signing, verification, keyless signing, and OCI registry storage.

claims.claim_text: Fulcio issues short-lived certificates for keyless signing tied to OIDC identities.
claims.span_hint: line 106 | section: 2. Establish Artifact Identity with Digests and Signatures
claims.rationale: The Fulcio README states that Fulcio issues code-signing certificates for OIDC identities and that certificates are short-lived.

claims.claim_text: Rekor records signature metadata in a transparency log that can be queried or audited later.
claims.span_hint: line 106 | section: 2. Establish Artifact Identity with Digests and Signatures
claims.rationale: The Rekor README describes Rekor as a transparency log server for storage, verification, inclusion proof, and retrieval of entries.

claims.claim_text: An SBOM is a formal inventory of software components and supply-chain relationships, useful for vulnerability identification and remediation.
claims.span_hint: line 171 | section: 3. Add SBOMs and Vulnerability Scans as Evidence, Not Decoration
claims.rationale: NIST cites EO 14028's SBOM definition and explains SBOM value for transparency and vulnerability remediation.

claims.claim_text: SPDX is an SBOM standard with common serializations including JSON, RDF, and tag-value.
claims.span_hint: lines 177-182 | section: 3. Add SBOMs and Vulnerability Scans as Evidence, SBOM FORMATS diagram
claims.rationale: The exact SPDX serialization details are documented by the official SPDX specification site, whose host is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CycloneDX is an OWASP Bill of Materials standard with JSON and XML media types.
claims.span_hint: lines 183-187 | section: 3. Add SBOMs and Vulnerability Scans as Evidence, SBOM FORMATS diagram
claims.rationale: The CycloneDX specification repository identifies CycloneDX as an OWASP BOM standard and lists official JSON and XML media types.

claims.claim_text: Syft generates SBOMs from container images, filesystems, and directories.
claims.span_hint: line 193 | section: 3. Add SBOMs and Vulnerability Scans as Evidence, Not Decoration
claims.rationale: The Syft README describes Syft as a CLI and library for generating SBOMs from container images and filesystems.

claims.claim_text: Trivy can generate SBOMs and scan images, filesystems, configuration files, and existing SBOMs.
claims.span_hint: line 193 | section: 3. Add SBOMs and Vulnerability Scans as Evidence, Not Decoration
claims.rationale: The Trivy README lists vulnerability, misconfiguration, secret, and SBOM scanning across containers, Kubernetes, repositories, clouds, and related targets.

claims.claim_text: Harbor is a CNCF graduated cloud native registry project.
claims.span_hint: line 259 | section: 4. Use Registries and Admission Control as Enforcement Points
claims.rationale: CNCF's Harbor project page identifies Harbor as a registry project and states that it moved to Graduated maturity.

claims.claim_text: Harbor provides registry features such as project access control, vulnerability scanning, replication, auditing, garbage collection, and signing integration.
claims.span_hint: line 259 | section: 4. Use Registries and Admission Control as Enforcement Points
claims.rationale: Harbor's README lists role-based access control, replication, vulnerability scanning, image deletion and garbage collection, Notary signing support, and auditing.

claims.claim_text: Ratify is a Kubernetes artifact verification engine that admits deployments only when artifact security metadata complies with policy.
claims.span_hint: line 332 | section: 4. Use Registries and Admission Control as Enforcement Points
claims.rationale: The Ratify README describes Ratify as a verification engine for artifact security metadata that admits deployments only when policies are satisfied.

claims.claim_text: Gatekeeper provides Kubernetes policy constraints, audit functionality, and external data support.
claims.span_hint: line 332 | section: 4. Use Registries and Admission Control as Enforcement Points
claims.rationale: Gatekeeper's README lists native Kubernetes constraints, constraint templates, mutation support, audit functionality, and external data support.

claims.claim_text: Cosign stores signature objects alongside container artifacts in OCI-compatible registry storage.
claims.span_hint: line 442 | section: 5. Scaffold from Single Controls to a Complete Pipeline
claims.rationale: The Cosign README explicitly lists container signing, verification, and storage in an OCI registry.

claims.claim_text: SLSA is a software supply-chain security framework for reasoning about build provenance and increasing supply-chain integrity.
claims.span_hint: line 579 | section: 6. Operate the System: Exceptions, Provenance, and Incident Response
claims.rationale: The SLSA repository defines SLSA as Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts and as a framework for increasing software security and supply-chain integrity.

claims.claim_text: in-toto provides a framework for protecting software supply-chain integrity by verifying planned steps and authorized functionaries.
claims.span_hint: line 579 | section: 6. Operate the System: Exceptions, Provenance, and Incident Response
claims.rationale: The in-toto README describes verification of planned supply-chain tasks by authorized personnel and protection against tampering in transit.

further_reading.title: sigstore/cosign
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for signing, verification, attestations, keyless signing, and OCI registry storage used throughout the module.

further_reading.title: NIST SBOM Guidance
further_reading.why_relevant: Authoritative background on SBOM definition, minimum elements, standard formats, and vulnerability-response value.

further_reading.title: SLSA Framework
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for provenance-oriented supply-chain maturity and build integrity concepts.

further_reading.title: Harbor Registry
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary project reference for Harbor registry features including scanning, signing integration, RBAC, replication, and auditing.

notes: No rewrite dispositions were needed for the supplied module text. Repeated Did You Know and Common Mistakes statements are covered by earlier claims; quiz prompts, exercises, code blocks, and purely pedagogical flow were not emitted as separate claims.
